qa.question: What is the asymptotic Bartlett rejection rate at T=50 with rho=0.8?
What is the wild bootstrap rejection rate at T=50 with rho=0.8?
qa.answer: 7.4, much closer to the 5% nominal level than the asymptotic rate of 14.2

What is the MA coefficient in DGP 3?
What is the slope parameter under the null across all DGPs?

equations.label: eq:hac
equations.description: HAC variance estimator with kernel $k(\cdot)$ and bandwidth $M_T$. Autocovariance $\hat{\Gamma}(j)$ uses OLS residuals $\hat{u}_t$.

qa.question: What does the bandwidth M_T control in the HAC estimator?
qa.answer: The truncation point: autocovariances at lag |j| > M_T receive zero weight

equations.label: eq:bartlett
equations.description: Bartlett (tent) kernel used in Newey-West HAC estimator. Compactly supported and guarantees non-negative weights on autocovariances.

qa.question: What is the Bartlett kernel value at u=0.5?

equations.label: eq:nw
equations.description: Newey-West sandwich variance estimator. $\hat{Q}_{xx} = T^{-1}\sum x_t x_t'$ is the sample second moment matrix.

qa.question: What is the weight on autocovariance at lag j in the Newey-West formula?

equations.label: eq:consistency
equations.description: Consistency conditions on bandwidth $M_T$: must grow with $T$ to capture all relevant autocovariances, but grow slower than $T^{1/2}$ to ensure averaging reduces variance.

qa.question: What conditions on bandwidth M_T ensure HAC consistency?

equations.label: eq:nw-bw
equations.description: Newey-West (1994) bandwidth rule: a deterministic function of sample size $T$. Grows at rate $O(T^{2/9})$, satisfying $M_T \to \infty$ and $M_T / T^{1/2} \to 0$.

equations.label: eq:mse
equations.description: MSE decomposition: variance term grows as $M_T / T$; bias term decays as $M_T^{-2q}$ where $q$ is the kernel characteristic exponent.

equations.label: eq:opt-bw
equations.description: Andrews (1991) MSE-optimal bandwidth formula, where $c_k$ is a kernel-specific constant, $q$ is the kernel characteristic exponent, and $\hat{\alpha}(q)$ is a nuisance parameter estimated from data.

qa.question: What does the kernel characteristic exponent q determine?
qa.answer: The rate at which bias decays with bandwidth: bias = O(M_T^{-2q})

equations.label: eq:prewhiten
equations.description: Pre-whitened HAC estimator: fit AR(1) to residuals, apply HAC to AR residuals $\hat{v}_t$, then rescale by $(1-\hat{\phi})^{-2}$.

qa.question: What conditions on bandwidth M_T ensure HAC consistency?
Which kernel has the highest asymptotic efficiency?
qa.answer: Quadratic Spectral (QS) kernel with efficiency 1.421 relative to Bartlett
